2383 // Call locate module callback if set. This allows users to implement their
2384 // own module cache system. For example, to leverage build system artifacts,
2385 // to bypass pulling files from remote platform, or to search symbol files
2386 // from symbol servers.
2387 if (m_platform_sp)
2388 m_platform_sp->CallLocateModuleCallbackIfSet(
2389 module_spec, module_sp, symbol_file_spec, &did_create_module);
2390
2391 // The result of this CallLocateModuleCallbackIfSet is one of the following.
2392 // 1. module_sp:loaded, symbol_file_spec:set
2393 // The callback found a module file and a symbol file for the
2394 // module_spec. We will call module_sp->SetSymbolFileFileSpec with
2395 // the symbol_file_spec later.
2396 // 2. module_sp:loaded, symbol_file_spec:empty
2397 // The callback only found a module file for the module_spec.
2398 // 3. module_sp:empty, symbol_file_spec:set
2399 // The callback only found a symbol file for the module. We continue
2400 // to find a module file for this module_spec and we will call
2401 // module_sp->SetSymbolFileFileSpec with the symbol_file_spec later.
2402 // 4. module_sp:empty, symbol_file_spec:empty
2403 // Platform does not exist, the callback is not set, the callback did
2404 // not find any module files nor any symbol files, the callback failed,
2405 // or something went wrong. We continue to find a module file for this
2406 // module_spec.
2407
2408 if (!module_sp) {
2409 // If there are image search path entries, try to use them to acquire a
2410 // suitable image.
2411 if (m_image_search_paths.GetSize()) {
2412 ModuleSpec transformed_spec(module_spec);
2413 ConstString transformed_dir;
2414 if (m_image_search_paths.RemapPath(
2415 module_spec.GetFileSpec().GetDirectory(), transformed_dir)) {
2416 transformed_spec.GetFileSpec().SetDirectory(transformed_dir);
2417 transformed_spec.GetFileSpec().SetFilename(
2418 module_spec.GetFileSpec().GetFilename());
2419 transformed_spec.SetTarget(shared_from_this());
2420 error = ModuleList::GetSharedModule(transformed_spec, module_sp,
2421 &old_modules, &did_create_module);
2422 }
2423 }
2424 }
2425
2426 if (!module_sp) {
2427 // If we have a UUID, we can check our global shared module list in case
2428 // we already have it. If we don't have a valid UUID, then we can't since
2429 // the path in "module_spec" will be a platform path, and we will need to
2430 // let the platform find that file. For example, we could be asking for
2431 // "/usr/lib/dyld" and if we do not have a UUID, we don't want to pick
2432 // the local copy of "/usr/lib/dyld" since our platform could be a remote
2433 // platform that has its own "/usr/lib/dyld" in an SDK or in a local file
2434 // cache.
2435 if (module_spec.GetUUID().IsValid()) {
2436 // We have a UUID, it is OK to check the global module list...
2437 error = ModuleList::GetSharedModule(module_spec, module_sp,
2438 &old_modules, &did_create_module);
2439 }
2440
2441 if (!module_sp) {
2442 // The platform is responsible for finding and caching an appropriate
2443 // module in the shared module cache.
2444 if (m_platform_sp) {
2445 error = m_platform_sp->GetSharedModule(
2446 module_spec, m_process_sp.get(), module_sp, &old_modules,
2447 &did_create_module);
2448 } else {
2449 error = Status::FromErrorString("no platform is currently set");
2450 }
2451 }
2452 }
2453
2454 // We found a module that wasn't in our target list. Let's make sure that
2455 // there wasn't an equivalent module in the list already, and if there was,
2456 // let's remove it.
2457 if (module_sp) {
2458 ObjectFile *objfile = module_sp->GetObjectFile();
2459 if (objfile) {
2460 switch (objfile->GetType()) {
2461 case ObjectFile::eTypeCoreFile: /// A core file that has a checkpoint of
2462 /// a program's execution state
2463 case ObjectFile::eTypeExecutable: /// A normal executable
2464 case ObjectFile::eTypeDynamicLinker: /// The platform's dynamic linker
2465 /// executable
2466 case ObjectFile::eTypeObjectFile: /// An intermediate object file
2467 case ObjectFile::eTypeSharedLibrary: /// A shared library that can be
2468 /// used during execution
2469 break;
2470 case ObjectFile::eTypeDebugInfo: /// An object file that contains only
2471 /// debug information
2472 if (error_ptr)
2473 *error_ptr = Status::FromErrorString(
2474 "debug info files aren't valid target "
2475 "modules, please specify an executable");
2476 return ModuleSP();
2477 case ObjectFile::eTypeStubLibrary: /// A library that can be linked
2478 /// against but not used for
2479 /// execution
2480 if (error_ptr)
2481 *error_ptr = Status::FromErrorString(
2482 "stub libraries aren't valid target "
2483 "modules, please specify an executable");
2484 return ModuleSP();
2485 default:
2486 if (error_ptr)
2487 *error_ptr = Status::FromErrorString(
2488 "unsupported file type, please specify an executable");
2489 return ModuleSP();
2490 }
2491 // GetSharedModule is not guaranteed to find the old shared module, for
2492 // instance in the common case where you pass in the UUID, it is only
2493 // going to find the one module matching the UUID. In fact, it has no
2494 // good way to know what the "old module" relevant to this target is,
2495 // since there might be many copies of a module with this file spec in
2496 // various running debug sessions, but only one of them will belong to
2497 // this target. So let's remove the UUID from the module list, and look
2498 // in the target's module list. Only do this if there is SOMETHING else
2499 // in the module spec...
2500 if (module_spec.GetUUID().IsValid() &&
2501 !module_spec.GetFileSpec().GetFilename().IsEmpty() &&
2502 !module_spec.GetFileSpec().GetDirectory().IsEmpty()) {
2503 ModuleSpec module_spec_copy(module_spec.GetFileSpec());
2504 module_spec_copy.GetUUID().Clear();
2505
2506 ModuleList found_modules;
2507 m_images.FindModules(module_spec_copy, found_modules);
2508 found_modules.ForEach([&](const ModuleSP &found_module) {
2509 old_modules.push_back(found_module);
2510 return IterationAction::Continue;
2511 });
2512 }
2513
2514 // If the locate module callback had found a symbol file, set it to the
2515 // module_sp before preloading symbols.
2516 if (symbol_file_spec)
2517 module_sp->SetSymbolFileFileSpec(symbol_file_spec);
2518
2519 llvm::SmallVector<ModuleSP, 1> replaced_modules;
2520 for (ModuleSP &old_module_sp : old_modules) {
2521 if (m_images.GetIndexForModule(old_module_sp.get()) !=
2522 LLDB_INVALID_INDEX32) {
2523 if (replaced_modules.empty())
2524 m_images.ReplaceModule(old_module_sp, module_sp);
2525 else
2526 m_images.Remove(old_module_sp);
2527
2528 replaced_modules.push_back(std::move(old_module_sp));
2529 }
2530 }
2531
2532 if (replaced_modules.size() > 1) {
2533 // The same new module replaced multiple old modules
2534 // simultaneously. It's not clear this should ever
2535 // happen (if we always replace old modules as we add
2536 // new ones, presumably we should never have more than
2537 // one old one). If there are legitimate cases where
2538 // this happens, then the ModuleList::Notifier interface
2539 // may need to be adjusted to allow reporting this.
2540 // In the meantime, just log that this has happened; just
2541 // above we called ReplaceModule on the first one, and Remove
2542 // on the rest.
2543 if (Log *log = GetLog(LLDBLog::Target | LLDBLog::Modules)) {
2544 StreamString message;
2545 auto dump = [&message](Module &dump_module) -> void {
2546 UUID dump_uuid = dump_module.GetUUID();
2547
2548 message << '[';
2549 dump_module.GetDescription(message.AsRawOstream());
2550 message << " (uuid ";
2551
2552 if (dump_uuid.IsValid())
2553 dump_uuid.Dump(message);
2554 else
2555 message << "not specified";
2556
2557 message << ")]";
2558 };
2559
2560 message << "New module ";
2561 dump(*module_sp);
2562 message.AsRawOstream()
2563 << llvm::formatv(" simultaneously replaced {0} old modules: ",
2564 replaced_modules.size());
2565 for (ModuleSP &replaced_module_sp : replaced_modules)
2566 dump(*replaced_module_sp);
2567
2568 log->PutString(message.GetString());
2569 }
2570 }
2571
2572 if (replaced_modules.empty())
2573 m_images.Append(module_sp, notify);
2574
2575 for (ModuleSP &old_module_sp : replaced_modules) {
2576 auto old_module_wp = old_module_sp->weak_from_this();
2577 old_module_sp.reset();
2578 ModuleList::RemoveSharedModuleIfOrphaned(old_module_wp);
2579 }
2580 } else
2581 module_sp.reset();
2582 }
2583 }
2584 if (error_ptr)
2585 *error_ptr = std::move(error);
2586 return module_sp;
2587}
2588
2589TargetSP Target::CalculateTarget() { return shared_from_this(); }
2590
2591ProcessSP Target::CalculateProcess() { return m_process_sp; }
2592
2593ThreadSP Target::CalculateThread() { return ThreadSP(); }
2594
2595StackFrameSP Target::CalculateStackFrame() { return StackFrameSP(); }
2596
2597void Target::CalculateExecutionContext(ExecutionContext &exe_ctx) {
2598 exe_ctx.Clear();
2599 exe_ctx.SetTargetPtr(this);
2600}
2601
2602PathMappingList &Target::GetImageSearchPathList() {
2603 return m_image_search_paths;
2604}
2605
2606void Target::ImageSearchPathsChanged(const PathMappingList &path_list,
2607 void *baton) {
2608 Target *target = (Target *)baton;
2609 ModuleSP exe_module_sp(target->GetExecutableModule());
2610 if (exe_module_sp)
2611 target->SetExecutableModule(exe_module_sp, eLoadDependentsYes);
2612}
2613
2614llvm::Expected<lldb::TypeSystemSP>
2615Target::GetScratchTypeSystemForLanguage(lldb::LanguageType language,
2616 bool create_on_demand) {
2617 if (!m_valid)
2618 return llvm::createStringError("Invalid Target");
2619
2620 if (language == eLanguageTypeMipsAssembler // GNU AS and LLVM use it for all
2621 // assembly code
2622 || language == eLanguageTypeUnknown) {
2623 LanguageSet languages_for_expressions =
2624 Language::GetLanguagesSupportingTypeSystemsForExpressions();
2625
2626 if (languages_for_expressions[eLanguageTypeC]) {
2627 language = eLanguageTypeC; // LLDB's default. Override by setting the
2628 // target language.
2629 } else {
2630 if (languages_for_expressions.Empty())
2631 return llvm::createStringError(
2632 "No expression support for any languages");
2633 language = (LanguageType)languages_for_expressions.bitvector.find_first();
2634 }
2635 }
2636
2637 return m_scratch_type_system_map.GetTypeSystemForLanguage(language, this,
2638 create_on_demand);
2639}
2640
2641CompilerType Target::GetRegisterType(const std::string &name,
2642 const lldb_private::RegisterFlags &flags,
2643 uint32_t byte_size) {
2644 RegisterTypeBuilderSP provider = PluginManager::GetRegisterTypeBuilder(*this);
2645 assert(provider);
2646 return provider->GetRegisterType(name, flags, byte_size);
2647}
2648
2649std::vector<lldb::TypeSystemSP>
2650Target::GetScratchTypeSystems(bool create_on_demand) {
2651 if (!m_valid)
2652 return {};
2653
2654 // Some TypeSystem instances are associated with several LanguageTypes so
2655 // they will show up several times in the loop below. The SetVector filters
2656 // out all duplicates as they serve no use for the caller.
2657 std::vector<lldb::TypeSystemSP> scratch_type_systems;
2658
2659 LanguageSet languages_for_expressions =
2660 Language::GetLanguagesSupportingTypeSystemsForExpressions();
2661
2662 for (auto bit : languages_for_expressions.bitvector.set_bits()) {
2663 auto language = (LanguageType)bit;
2664 auto type_system_or_err =
2665 GetScratchTypeSystemForLanguage(language, create_on_demand);
2666 if (!type_system_or_err)
2667 LLDB_LOG_ERROR(
2668 GetLog(LLDBLog::Target), type_system_or_err.takeError(),
2669 "Language '{1}' has expression support but no scratch type "
2670 "system available: {0}",
2671 Language::GetNameForLanguageType(language));
2672 else
2673 if (auto ts = *type_system_or_err)
2674 scratch_type_systems.push_back(ts);
2675 }
2676
2677 std::sort(scratch_type_systems.begin(), scratch_type_systems.end());
2678 scratch_type_systems.erase(llvm::unique(scratch_type_systems),
2679 scratch_type_systems.end());
2680 return scratch_type_systems;
2681}
2682
2683PersistentExpressionState *
2684Target::GetPersistentExpressionStateForLanguage(lldb::LanguageType language) {
2685 auto type_system_or_err = GetScratchTypeSystemForLanguage(language, true);
2686
2687 if (auto err = type_system_or_err.takeError()) {
2688 LLDB_LOG_ERROR(
2689 GetLog(LLDBLog::Target), std::move(err),
2690 "Unable to get persistent expression state for language {1}: {0}",
2691 Language::GetNameForLanguageType(language));
2692 return nullptr;
2693 }
2694
2695 if (auto ts = *type_system_or_err)
2696 return ts->GetPersistentExpressionState();
2697
2698 LLDB_LOG(GetLog(LLDBLog::Target),
2699 "Unable to get persistent expression state for language {1}: {0}",
2700 Language::GetNameForLanguageType(language));
2701 return nullptr;
2702}
2703
2704UserExpression *Target::GetUserExpressionForLanguage(
2705 llvm::StringRef expr, llvm::StringRef prefix, SourceLanguage language,
2706 Expression::ResultType desired_type,
2707 const EvaluateExpressionOptions &options, ValueObject *ctx_obj,
2708 Status &error) {
2709 auto type_system_or_err =
2710 GetScratchTypeSystemForLanguage(language.AsLanguageType());
2711 if (auto err = type_system_or_err.takeError()) {
2712 error = Status::FromErrorStringWithFormat(
2713 "Could not find type system for language %s: %s",
2714 Language::GetNameForLanguageType(language.AsLanguageType()),
2715 llvm::toString(std::move(err)).c_str());
2716 return nullptr;
2717 }
2718
2719 auto ts = *type_system_or_err;
2720 if (!ts) {
2721 error = Status::FromErrorStringWithFormat(
2722 "Type system for language %s is no longer live",
2723 language.GetDescription().data());
2724 return nullptr;
2725 }
2726
2727 auto *user_expr = ts->GetUserExpression(expr, prefix, language, desired_type,
2728 options, ctx_obj);
2729 if (!user_expr)
2730 error = Status::FromErrorStringWithFormat(
2731 "Could not create an expression for language %s",
2732 language.GetDescription().data());
2733
2734 return user_expr;
2735}
2736
2737FunctionCaller *Target::GetFunctionCallerForLanguage(
2738 lldb::LanguageType language, const CompilerType &return_type,
2739 const Address &function_address, const ValueList &arg_value_list,
2740 const char *name, Status &error) {
2741 auto type_system_or_err = GetScratchTypeSystemForLanguage(language);
2742 if (auto err = type_system_or_err.takeError()) {
2743 error = Status::FromErrorStringWithFormat(
2744 "Could not find type system for language %s: %s",
2745 Language::GetNameForLanguageType(language),
2746 llvm::toString(std::move(err)).c_str());
2747 return nullptr;
2748 }
2749 auto ts = *type_system_or_err;
2750 if (!ts) {
2751 error = Status::FromErrorStringWithFormat(
2752 "Type system for language %s is no longer live",
2753 Language::GetNameForLanguageType(language));
2754 return nullptr;
2755 }
2756 auto *persistent_fn = ts->GetFunctionCaller(return_type, function_address,
2757 arg_value_list, name);
2758 if (!persistent_fn)
2759 error = Status::FromErrorStringWithFormat(
2760 "Could not create an expression for language %s",
2761 Language::GetNameForLanguageType(language));
2762
2763 return persistent_fn;
2764}
2765
2766llvm::Expected<std::unique_ptr<UtilityFunction>>
2767Target::CreateUtilityFunction(std::string expression, std::string name,
2768 lldb::LanguageType language,
2769 ExecutionContext &exe_ctx) {
2770 auto type_system_or_err = GetScratchTypeSystemForLanguage(language);
2771 if (!type_system_or_err)
2772 return type_system_or_err.takeError();
2773 auto ts = *type_system_or_err;
2774 if (!ts)
2775 return llvm::createStringError(
2776 llvm::StringRef("Type system for language ") +
2777 Language::GetNameForLanguageType(language) +
2778 llvm::StringRef(" is no longer live"));
2779 std::unique_ptr<UtilityFunction> utility_fn =
2780 ts->CreateUtilityFunction(std::move(expression), std::move(name));
2781 if (!utility_fn)
2782 return llvm::createStringError(
2783 llvm::StringRef("Could not create an expression for language") +
2784 Language::GetNameForLanguageType(language));
2785
2786 DiagnosticManager diagnostics;
2787 if (!utility_fn->Install(diagnostics, exe_ctx))
2788 return diagnostics.GetAsError(lldb::eExpressionSetupError,
2789 "Could not install utility function:");
2790
2791 return std::move(utility_fn);
2792}
2793
2794void Target::SettingsInitialize() { Process::SettingsInitialize(); }
2795
2796void Target::SettingsTerminate() { Process::SettingsTerminate(); }
2797
2798FileSpecList Target::GetDefaultExecutableSearchPaths() {
2799 return Target::GetGlobalProperties().GetExecutableSearchPaths();
2800}
2801
2802FileSpecList Target::GetDefaultDebugFileSearchPaths() {
2803 return Target::GetGlobalProperties().GetDebugFileSearchPaths();
2804}
2805
2806ArchSpec Target::GetDefaultArchitecture() {
2807 return Target::GetGlobalProperties().GetDefaultArchitecture();
2808}
2809
2810void Target::SetDefaultArchitecture(const ArchSpec &arch) {
2811 LLDB_LOG(GetLog(LLDBLog::Target),
2812 "setting target's default architecture to {0} ({1})",
2813 arch.GetArchitectureName(), arch.GetTriple().getTriple());
2814 Target::GetGlobalProperties().SetDefaultArchitecture(arch);
2815}
2816
2817llvm::Error Target::SetLabel(llvm::StringRef label) {
2818 size_t n = LLDB_INVALID_INDEX32;
2819 if (llvm::to_integer(label, n))
2820 return llvm::createStringError("Cannot use integer as target label.");
2821 TargetList &targets = GetDebugger().GetTargetList();
2822 for (size_t i = 0; i < targets.GetNumTargets(); i++) {
2823 TargetSP target_sp = targets.GetTargetAtIndex(i);
2824 if (target_sp && target_sp->GetLabel() == label) {
2825 return llvm::make_error<llvm::StringError>(
2826 llvm::formatv(
2827 "Cannot use label '{0}' since it's set in target #{1}.", label,
2828 i),
2829 llvm::inconvertibleErrorCode());
2830 }
2831 }
2832
2833 m_label = label.str();
2834 return llvm::Error::success();
2835}
2836
2837Target *Target::GetTargetFromContexts(const ExecutionContext *exe_ctx_ptr,
2838 const SymbolContext *sc_ptr) {
2839 // The target can either exist in the "process" of ExecutionContext, or in
2840 // the "target_sp" member of SymbolContext. This accessor helper function
2841 // will get the target from one of these locations.
2842
2843 Target *target = nullptr;
2844 if (sc_ptr != nullptr)
2845 target = sc_ptr->target_sp.get();
2846 if (target == nullptr && exe_ctx_ptr)
2847 target = exe_ctx_ptr->GetTargetPtr();
2848 return target;
2849}
2850
2851ExpressionResults Target::EvaluateExpression(
2852 llvm::StringRef expr, ExecutionContextScope *exe_scope,
2853 lldb::ValueObjectSP &result_valobj_sp,
2854 const EvaluateExpressionOptions &options, std::string *fixed_expression,
2855 ValueObject *ctx_obj) {
2856 result_valobj_sp.reset();
2857
2858 ExpressionResults execution_results = eExpressionSetupError;
2859
2860 if (expr.empty()) {
2861 m_stats.GetExpressionStats().NotifyFailure();
2862 return execution_results;
2863 }
2864
2865 // We shouldn't run stop hooks in expressions.
2866 bool old_suppress_value = m_suppress_stop_hooks;
2867 m_suppress_stop_hooks = true;
2868 llvm::scope_exit on_exit([this, old_suppress_value]() {
2869 m_suppress_stop_hooks = old_suppress_value;
2870 });
2871
2872 ExecutionContext exe_ctx;
2873
2874 if (exe_scope) {
2875 exe_scope->CalculateExecutionContext(exe_ctx);
2876 } else if (m_process_sp) {
2877 m_process_sp->CalculateExecutionContext(exe_ctx);
2878 } else {
2879 CalculateExecutionContext(exe_ctx);
2880 }
2881
2882 // Make sure we aren't just trying to see the value of a persistent variable
2883 // (something like "$0")
2884 // Only check for persistent variables the expression starts with a '$'
2885 lldb::ExpressionVariableSP persistent_var_sp;
2886 if (expr[0] == '$') {
2887 auto type_system_or_err =
2888 GetScratchTypeSystemForLanguage(eLanguageTypeC);
2889 if (auto err = type_system_or_err.takeError()) {
2890 LLDB_LOG_ERROR(GetLog(LLDBLog::Target), std::move(err),
2891 "Unable to get scratch type system");
2892 } else {
2893 auto ts = *type_system_or_err;
2894 if (!ts)
2895 LLDB_LOG_ERROR(GetLog(LLDBLog::Target), std::move(err),
2896 "Scratch type system is no longer live: {0}");
2897 else
2898 persistent_var_sp =
2899 ts->GetPersistentExpressionState()->GetVariable(expr);
2900 }
2901 }
2902 if (persistent_var_sp) {
2903 result_valobj_sp = persistent_var_sp->GetValueObject();
2904 execution_results = eExpressionCompleted;
2905 } else {
2906 llvm::StringRef prefix = GetExpressionPrefixContents();
2907 execution_results =
2908 UserExpression::Evaluate(exe_ctx, options, expr, prefix,
2909 result_valobj_sp, fixed_expression, ctx_obj);
2910 }
2911
2912 if (execution_results == eExpressionCompleted)
2913 m_stats.GetExpressionStats().NotifySuccess();
2914 else
2915 m_stats.GetExpressionStats().NotifyFailure();
2916 return execution_results;
2917}
2918
2919lldb::ExpressionVariableSP Target::GetPersistentVariable(ConstString name) {
2920 lldb::ExpressionVariableSP variable_sp;
2921 m_scratch_type_system_map.ForEach(
2922 [name, &variable_sp](TypeSystemSP type_system) -> bool {
2923 auto ts = type_system.get();
2924 if (!ts)
2925 return true;
2926 if (PersistentExpressionState *persistent_state =
2927 ts->GetPersistentExpressionState()) {
2928 variable_sp = persistent_state->GetVariable(name);
2929
2930 if (variable_sp)
2931 return false; // Stop iterating the ForEach
2932 }
2933 return true; // Keep iterating the ForEach
2934 });
2935 return variable_sp;
2936}
2937
2938lldb::addr_t Target::GetPersistentSymbol(ConstString name) {
2939 lldb::addr_t address = LLDB_INVALID_ADDRESS;
2940
2941 m_scratch_type_system_map.ForEach(
2942 [name, &address](lldb::TypeSystemSP type_system) -> bool {
2943 auto ts = type_system.get();
2944 if (!ts)
2945 return true;
2946
2947 if (PersistentExpressionState *persistent_state =
2948 ts->GetPersistentExpressionState()) {
2949 address = persistent_state->LookupSymbol(name);
2950 if (address != LLDB_INVALID_ADDRESS)
2951 return false; // Stop iterating the ForEach
2952 }
2953 return true; // Keep iterating the ForEach
2954 });
2955 return address;
2956}
2957
2958llvm::Expected<lldb_private::Address> Target::GetEntryPointAddress() {
2959 Module *exe_module = GetExecutableModulePointer();
2960
2961 // Try to find the entry point address in the primary executable.
2962 const bool has_primary_executable = exe_module && exe_module->GetObjectFile();
2963 if (has_primary_executable) {
2964 Address entry_addr = exe_module->GetObjectFile()->GetEntryPointAddress();
2965 if (entry_addr.IsValid())
2966 return entry_addr;
2967 }
2968
2969 const ModuleList &modules = GetImages();
2970 const size_t num_images = modules.GetSize();
2971 for (size_t idx = 0; idx < num_images; ++idx) {
2972 ModuleSP module_sp(modules.GetModuleAtIndex(idx));
2973 if (!module_sp || !module_sp->GetObjectFile())
2974 continue;
2975
2976 Address entry_addr = module_sp->GetObjectFile()->GetEntryPointAddress();
2977 if (entry_addr.IsValid())
2978 return entry_addr;
2979 }
2980
2981 // We haven't found the entry point address. Return an appropriate error.
2982 if (!has_primary_executable)
2983 return llvm::createStringError(
2984 "No primary executable found and could not find entry point address in "
2985 "any executable module");
2986
2987 return llvm::createStringError(
2988 "Could not find entry point address for primary executable module \"" +
2989 exe_module->GetFileSpec().GetFilename().GetStringRef() + "\"");
2990}
2991
2992lldb::addr_t Target::GetCallableLoadAddress(lldb::addr_t load_addr,
2993 AddressClass addr_class) const {
2994 auto arch_plugin = GetArchitecturePlugin();
2995 return arch_plugin
2996 ? arch_plugin->GetCallableLoadAddress(load_addr, addr_class)
2997 : load_addr;
2998}
2999
3000lldb::addr_t Target::GetOpcodeLoadAddress(lldb::addr_t load_addr,
3001 AddressClass addr_class) const {
3002 auto arch_plugin = GetArchitecturePlugin();
3003 return arch_plugin ? arch_plugin->GetOpcodeLoadAddress(load_addr, addr_class)
3004 : load_addr;
3005}
3006
3007lldb::addr_t Target::GetBreakableLoadAddress(lldb::addr_t addr) {
3008 auto arch_plugin = GetArchitecturePlugin();
3009 return arch_plugin ? arch_plugin->GetBreakableLoadAddress(addr, *this) : addr;
3010}
3011
3012llvm::Expected<lldb::DisassemblerSP>
3013Target::ReadInstructions(const Address &start_addr, uint32_t count,
3014 const char *flavor_string) {
3015 DataBufferHeap data(GetArchitecture().GetMaximumOpcodeByteSize() * count, 0);
3016 bool force_live_memory = true;
3017 lldb_private::Status error;
3018 lldb::addr_t load_addr = LLDB_INVALID_ADDRESS;
3019 const size_t bytes_read =
3020 ReadMemory(start_addr, data.GetBytes(), data.GetByteSize(), error,
3021 force_live_memory, &load_addr);
3022
3023 if (error.Fail())
3024 return llvm::createStringError(
3025 error.AsCString("Target::ReadInstructions failed to read memory at %s"),
3026 start_addr.GetLoadAddress(this));
3027
3028 const bool data_from_file = load_addr == LLDB_INVALID_ADDRESS;
3029 if (!flavor_string || flavor_string[0] == '\0') {
3030 // FIXME - we don't have the mechanism in place to do per-architecture
3031 // settings. But since we know that for now we only support flavors on
3032 // x86 & x86_64,
3033 const llvm::Triple::ArchType arch = GetArchitecture().GetTriple().getArch();
3034 if (arch == llvm::Triple::x86 || arch == llvm::Triple::x86_64)
3035 flavor_string = GetDisassemblyFlavor();
3036 }
3037
3038 return Disassembler::DisassembleBytes(
3039 GetArchitecture(), nullptr, flavor_string, GetDisassemblyCPU(),
3040 GetDisassemblyFeatures(), start_addr, data.GetBytes(), bytes_read, count,
3041 data_from_file);
3042}
3043
3044SourceManager &Target::GetSourceManager() {
3045 if (!m_source_manager_up)
3046 m_source_manager_up = std::make_unique<SourceManager>(shared_from_this());
3047 return *m_source_manager_up;
3048}
3049
3050Target::StopHookSP Target::CreateStopHook(StopHook::StopHookKind kind,
3051 bool internal) {
3052 user_id_t new_uid = (internal ? LLDB_INVALID_UID : ++m_stop_hook_next_id);
3053 Target::StopHookSP stop_hook_sp;
3054 switch (kind) {
3055 case StopHook::StopHookKind::CommandBased:
3056 stop_hook_sp.reset(new StopHookCommandLine(shared_from_this(), new_uid));
3057 break;
3058 case StopHook::StopHookKind::ScriptBased:
3059 stop_hook_sp.reset(new StopHookScripted(shared_from_this(), new_uid));
3060 break;
3061 case StopHook::StopHookKind::CodeBased:
3062 stop_hook_sp.reset(new StopHookCoded(shared_from_this(), new_uid));
3063 break;
3064 }
3065 if (internal)
3066 m_internal_stop_hooks.push_back(stop_hook_sp);
3067 else
3068 m_stop_hooks[new_uid] = stop_hook_sp;
3069 return stop_hook_sp;
3070}
3071
3072void Target::UndoCreateStopHook(lldb::user_id_t user_id) {
3073 if (!RemoveStopHookByID(user_id))
3074 return;
3075 if (user_id == m_stop_hook_next_id)
3076 m_stop_hook_next_id--;
3077}
3078
3079bool Target::RemoveStopHookByID(lldb::user_id_t user_id) {
3080 size_t num_removed = m_stop_hooks.erase(user_id);
3081 return (num_removed != 0);
3082}
3083
3084void Target::RemoveAllStopHooks() { m_stop_hooks.clear(); }
3085
3086Target::StopHookSP Target::GetStopHookByID(lldb::user_id_t user_id) {
3087 StopHookSP found_hook;
3088
3089 StopHookCollection::iterator specified_hook_iter;
3090 specified_hook_iter = m_stop_hooks.find(user_id);
3091 if (specified_hook_iter != m_stop_hooks.end())
3092 found_hook = (*specified_hook_iter).second;
3093 return found_hook;
3094}
3095
3096bool Target::SetStopHookActiveStateByID(lldb::user_id_t user_id,
3097 bool active_state) {
3098 StopHookCollection::iterator specified_hook_iter;
3099 specified_hook_iter = m_stop_hooks.find(user_id);
3100 if (specified_hook_iter == m_stop_hooks.end())
3101 return false;
3102
3103 (*specified_hook_iter).second->SetIsActive(active_state);
3104 return true;
3105}
3106
3107void Target::SetAllStopHooksActiveState(bool active_state) {
3108 StopHookCollection::iterator pos, end = m_stop_hooks.end();
3109 for (pos = m_stop_hooks.begin(); pos != end; pos++) {
3110 (*pos).second->SetIsActive(active_state);
3111 }
3112}
3113
3114// FIXME: Ideally we would like to return a `const &` (const reference) instead
3115// of creating copy here, but that is not possible due to different container
3116// types. In C++20, we should be able to use `std::ranges::views::values` to
3117// adapt the key-pair entries in the `std::map` (behind `StopHookCollection`)
3118// to avoid creating the copy.
3119const std::vector<Target::StopHookSP>
3120Target::GetStopHooks(bool internal) const {
3121 if (internal)
3122 return m_internal_stop_hooks;
3123
3124 std::vector<StopHookSP> stop_hooks;
3125 for (auto &[_, hook] : m_stop_hooks)
3126 stop_hooks.push_back(hook);
3127
3128 return stop_hooks;
3129}
3130
3131bool Target::RunStopHooks(bool at_initial_stop) {
3132 if (m_suppress_stop_hooks)
3133 return false;
3134
3135 if (!m_process_sp)
3136 return false;
3137
3138 // Somebody might have restarted the process:
3139 // Still return false, the return value is about US restarting the target.
3140 lldb::StateType state = m_process_sp->GetState();
3141 if (!(state == eStateStopped || state == eStateAttaching))
3142 return false;
3143
3144 auto is_active = [at_initial_stop](StopHookSP hook) {
3145 bool should_run_now = (!at_initial_stop || hook->GetRunAtInitialStop());
3146 return hook->IsActive() && should_run_now;
3147 };
3148
3149 // Create list of active internal and user stop hooks.
3150 std::vector<StopHookSP> active_hooks;
3151 llvm::copy_if(m_internal_stop_hooks, std::back_inserter(active_hooks),
3152 is_active);
3153 for (auto &[_, hook] : m_stop_hooks) {
3154 if (is_active(hook))
3155 active_hooks.push_back(hook);
3156 }
3157 if (active_hooks.empty())
3158 return false;
3159
3160 // Make sure we check that we are not stopped because of us running a user
3161 // expression since in that case we do not want to run the stop-hooks. Note,
3162 // you can't just check whether the last stop was for a User Expression,
3163 // because breakpoint commands get run before stop hooks, and one of them
3164 // might have run an expression. You have to ensure you run the stop hooks
3165 // once per natural stop.
3166 uint32_t last_natural_stop = m_process_sp->GetModIDRef().GetLastNaturalStopID();
3167 if (last_natural_stop != 0 && m_latest_stop_hook_id == last_natural_stop)
3168 return false;
3169
3170 m_latest_stop_hook_id = last_natural_stop;
3171
3172 std::vector<ExecutionContext> exc_ctx_with_reasons;
3173
3174 ThreadList &cur_threadlist = m_process_sp->GetThreadList();
3175 size_t num_threads = cur_threadlist.GetSize();
3176 for (size_t i = 0; i < num_threads; i++) {
3177 lldb::ThreadSP cur_thread_sp = cur_threadlist.GetThreadAtIndex(i);
3178 if (cur_thread_sp->ThreadStoppedForAReason()) {
3179 lldb::StackFrameSP cur_frame_sp = cur_thread_sp->GetStackFrameAtIndex(0);
3180 exc_ctx_with_reasons.emplace_back(m_process_sp.get(), cur_thread_sp.get(),
3181 cur_frame_sp.get());
3182 }
3183 }
3184
3185 // If no threads stopped for a reason, don't run the stop-hooks.
3186 // However, if this is the FIRST stop for this process, then we are in the
3187 // state where an attach or a core file load was completed without designating
3188 // a particular thread as responsible for the stop. In that case, we do
3189 // want to run the stop hooks, but do so just on one thread.
3190 size_t num_exe_ctx = exc_ctx_with_reasons.size();
3191 if (num_exe_ctx == 0) {
3192 if (at_initial_stop && num_threads > 0) {
3193 lldb::ThreadSP thread_to_use_sp = cur_threadlist.GetThreadAtIndex(0);
3194 exc_ctx_with_reasons.emplace_back(
3195 m_process_sp.get(), thread_to_use_sp.get(),
3196 thread_to_use_sp->GetStackFrameAtIndex(0).get());
3197 num_exe_ctx = 1;
3198 } else {
3199 return false;
3200 }
3201 }
3202
3203 StreamSP output_sp = m_debugger.GetAsyncOutputStream();
3204 llvm::scope_exit on_exit([output_sp] { output_sp->Flush(); });
3205
3206 size_t num_hooks_with_output = llvm::count_if(
3207 active_hooks, [](auto h) { return !h->GetSuppressOutput(); });
3208 bool print_hook_header = (num_hooks_with_output > 1);
3209 bool print_thread_header = (num_exe_ctx > 1);
3210 bool should_stop = false;
3211 bool requested_continue = false;
3212
3213 // A stop hook might get deleted while running stop hooks.
3214 // We have to decide what that means. We will follow the rule that deleting
3215 // a stop hook while processing these stop hooks will delete it for FUTURE
3216 // stops but not this stop. Fortunately, copying the m_stop_hooks to the
3217 // active_hooks list before iterating over the hooks has this effect.
3218 for (auto cur_hook_sp : active_hooks) {
3219 bool any_thread_matched = false;
3220 for (auto exc_ctx : exc_ctx_with_reasons) {
3221 if (!cur_hook_sp->ExecutionContextPasses(exc_ctx))
3222 continue;
3223
3224 bool suppress_output = cur_hook_sp->GetSuppressOutput();
3225 if (print_hook_header && !any_thread_matched && !suppress_output) {
3226 StreamString s;
3227 cur_hook_sp->GetDescription(s, eDescriptionLevelBrief);
3228 if (s.GetSize() != 0)
3229 output_sp->Printf("\n- Hook %" PRIu64 " (%s)\n", cur_hook_sp->GetID(),
3230 s.GetData());
3231 else
3232 output_sp->Printf("\n- Hook %" PRIu64 "\n", cur_hook_sp->GetID());
3233 any_thread_matched = true;
3234 }
3235
3236 if (print_thread_header && !suppress_output)
3237 output_sp->Printf("-- Thread %d\n",
3238 exc_ctx.GetThreadPtr()->GetIndexID());
3239
3240 auto result = cur_hook_sp->HandleStop(exc_ctx, output_sp);
3241 switch (result) {
3242 case StopHook::StopHookResult::KeepStopped:
3243 if (cur_hook_sp->GetAutoContinue())
3244 requested_continue = true;
3245 else
3246 should_stop = true;
3247 break;
3248 case StopHook::StopHookResult::RequestContinue:
3249 requested_continue = true;
3250 break;
3251 case StopHook::StopHookResult::NoPreference:
3252 // Do nothing
3253 break;
3254 case StopHook::StopHookResult::AlreadyContinued:
3255 // We don't have a good way to prohibit people from restarting the
3256 // target willy nilly in a stop hook. If the hook did so, give a
3257 // gentle suggestion here and back out of the hook processing.
3258 output_sp->Printf("\nAborting stop hooks, hook %" PRIu64
3259 " set the program running.\n"
3260 " Consider using '-G true' to make "
3261 "stop hooks auto-continue.\n",
3262 cur_hook_sp->GetID());
3263 // FIXME: if we are doing non-stop mode for real, we would have to
3264 // check that OUR thread was restarted, otherwise we should keep
3265 // processing stop hooks.
3266 return true;
3267 }
3268 }
3269 }
3270
3271 // Resume iff at least one hook requested to continue and no hook asked to
3272 // stop.
3273 if (requested_continue && !should_stop) {
3274 Log *log = GetLog(LLDBLog::Process);
3275 Status error = m_process_sp->PrivateResume();
3276 if (error.Success()) {
3277 LLDB_LOG(log, "Resuming from RunStopHooks");
3278 return true;
3279 } else {
3280 LLDB_LOG(log, "Resuming from RunStopHooks failed: {0}", error);
3281 return false;
3282 }
3283 }
3284
3285 return false;
3286}
3287
3288TargetProperties &Target::GetGlobalProperties() {
3289 // NOTE: intentional leak so we don't crash if global destructor chain gets
3290 // called as other threads still use the result of this function
3291 static TargetProperties *g_settings_ptr =
3292 new TargetProperties(nullptr);
3293 return *g_settings_ptr;
3294}
3295
3296Status Target::Install(ProcessLaunchInfo *launch_info) {
3297 Status error;
3298 PlatformSP platform_sp(GetPlatform());
3299 if (!platform_sp || !platform_sp->IsRemote() || !platform_sp->IsConnected())
3300 return error;
3301
3302 // Install all files that have an install path when connected to a
3303 // remote platform. If target.auto-install-main-executable is set then
3304 // also install the main executable even if it does not have an explicit
3305 // install path specified.
3306
3307 for (auto module_sp : GetImages().Modules()) {
3308 if (module_sp == GetExecutableModule()) {
3309 MainExecutableInstaller installer{platform_sp, module_sp,
3310 shared_from_this(), *launch_info};
3311 error = installExecutable(installer);
3312 } else {
3313 ExecutableInstaller installer{platform_sp, module_sp};
3314 error = installExecutable(installer);
3315 }
3316
3317 if (error.Fail())
3318 return error;
3319 }
3320
3321 return error;
3322}
3323
3324bool Target::ResolveLoadAddress(addr_t load_addr, Address &so_addr,
3325 uint32_t stop_id, bool allow_section_end) {
3326 return m_section_load_history.ResolveLoadAddress(stop_id, load_addr, so_addr,
3327 allow_section_end);
3328}
3329
3330bool Target::ResolveFileAddress(lldb::addr_t file_addr,
3331 Address &resolved_addr) {
3332 return m_images.ResolveFileAddress(file_addr, resolved_addr);
3333}
3334
3335bool Target::SetSectionLoadAddress(const SectionSP &section_sp,
3336 addr_t new_section_load_addr,
3337 bool warn_multiple) {
3338 const addr_t old_section_load_addr =
3339 m_section_load_history.GetSectionLoadAddress(
3340 SectionLoadHistory::eStopIDNow, section_sp);
3341 if (old_section_load_addr != new_section_load_addr) {
3342 uint32_t stop_id = 0;
3343 ProcessSP process_sp(GetProcessSP());
3344 if (process_sp)
3345 stop_id = process_sp->GetStopID();
3346 else
3347 stop_id = m_section_load_history.GetLastStopID();
3348 if (m_section_load_history.SetSectionLoadAddress(
3349 stop_id, section_sp, new_section_load_addr, warn_multiple))
3350 return true; // Return true if the section load address was changed...
3351 }
3352 return false; // Return false to indicate nothing changed
3353}
3354
3355size_t Target::UnloadModuleSections(const ModuleList &module_list) {
3356 size_t section_unload_count = 0;
3357 size_t num_modules = module_list.GetSize();
3358 for (size_t i = 0; i < num_modules; ++i) {
3359 section_unload_count +=
3360 UnloadModuleSections(module_list.GetModuleAtIndex(i));
3361 }
3362 return section_unload_count;
3363}
3364
3365size_t Target::UnloadModuleSections(const lldb::ModuleSP &module_sp) {
3366 uint32_t stop_id = 0;
3367 ProcessSP process_sp(GetProcessSP());
3368 if (process_sp)
3369 stop_id = process_sp->GetStopID();
3370 else
3371 stop_id = m_section_load_history.GetLastStopID();
3372 SectionList *sections = module_sp->GetSectionList();
3373 size_t section_unload_count = 0;
3374 if (sections) {
3375 const uint32_t num_sections = sections->GetNumSections(0);
3376 for (uint32_t i = 0; i < num_sections; ++i) {
3377 section_unload_count += m_section_load_history.SetSectionUnloaded(
3378 stop_id, sections->GetSectionAtIndex(i));
3379 }
3380 }
3381 return section_unload_count;
3382}
3383
3384bool Target::SetSectionUnloaded(const lldb::SectionSP &section_sp) {
3385 uint32_t stop_id = 0;
3386 ProcessSP process_sp(GetProcessSP());
3387 if (process_sp)
3388 stop_id = process_sp->GetStopID();
3389 else
3390 stop_id = m_section_load_history.GetLastStopID();
3391 return m_section_load_history.SetSectionUnloaded(stop_id, section_sp);
3392}
3393
3394bool Target::SetSectionUnloaded(const lldb::SectionSP &section_sp,
3395 addr_t load_addr) {
3396 uint32_t stop_id = 0;
3397 ProcessSP process_sp(GetProcessSP());
3398 if (process_sp)
3399 stop_id = process_sp->GetStopID();
3400 else
3401 stop_id = m_section_load_history.GetLastStopID();
3402 return m_section_load_history.SetSectionUnloaded(stop_id, section_sp,
3403 load_addr);
3404}
3405
3406void Target::ClearAllLoadedSections() { m_section_load_history.Clear(); }
3407
3408lldb_private::SummaryStatisticsSP Target::GetSummaryStatisticsSPForProviderName(
3409 lldb_private::TypeSummaryImpl &summary_provider) {
3410 return m_summary_statistics_cache.GetSummaryStatisticsForProvider(
3411 summary_provider);
3412}
3413
3414SummaryStatisticsCache &Target::GetSummaryStatisticsCache() {
3415 return m_summary_statistics_cache;
3416}
3417
3418void Target::SaveScriptedLaunchInfo(lldb_private::ProcessInfo &process_info) {
3419 if (process_info.IsScriptedProcess()) {
3420 // Only copy scripted process launch options.
3421 ProcessLaunchInfo &default_launch_info = const_cast<ProcessLaunchInfo &>(
3422 GetGlobalProperties().GetProcessLaunchInfo());
3423 default_launch_info.SetProcessPluginName("ScriptedProcess");
3424 default_launch_info.SetScriptedMetadata(process_info.GetScriptedMetadata());
3425 SetProcessLaunchInfo(default_launch_info);
3426 }
3427}
3428
3429Status Target::Launch(ProcessLaunchInfo &launch_info, Stream *stream) {
3430 m_stats.SetLaunchOrAttachTime();
3431 Status error;
3432 Log *log = GetLog(LLDBLog::Target);
3433
3434 LLDB_LOGF(log, "Target::%s() called for %s", __FUNCTION__,
3435 launch_info.GetExecutableFile().GetPath().c_str());
3436
3437 StateType state = eStateInvalid;
3438
3439 // Scope to temporarily get the process state in case someone has manually
3440 // remotely connected already to a process and we can skip the platform
3441 // launching.
3442 {
3443 ProcessSP process_sp(GetProcessSP());
3444
3445 if (process_sp) {
3446 state = process_sp->GetState();
3447 LLDB_LOGF(log,
3448 "Target::%s the process exists, and its current state is %s",
3449 __FUNCTION__, StateAsCString(state));
3450 } else {
3451 LLDB_LOGF(log, "Target::%s the process instance doesn't currently exist.",
3452 __FUNCTION__);
3453 }
3454 }
3455
3456 launch_info.GetFlags().Set(eLaunchFlagDebug);
3457
3458 SaveScriptedLaunchInfo(launch_info);
3459
3460 // Get the value of synchronous execution here. If you wait till after you
3461 // have started to run, then you could have hit a breakpoint, whose command
3462 // might switch the value, and then you'll pick up that incorrect value.
3463 Debugger &debugger = GetDebugger();
3464 const bool synchronous_execution =
3465 debugger.GetCommandInterpreter().GetSynchronous();
3466
3467 PlatformSP platform_sp(GetPlatform());
3468
3469 FinalizeFileActions(launch_info);
3470
3471 if (state == eStateConnected) {
3472 if (launch_info.GetFlags().Test(eLaunchFlagLaunchInTTY))
3473 return Status::FromErrorString(
3474 "can't launch in tty when launching through a remote connection");
3475 }
3476
3477 if (!launch_info.GetArchitecture().IsValid())
3478 launch_info.GetArchitecture() = GetArchitecture();
3479
3480 // Hijacking events of the process to be created to be sure that all events
3481 // until the first stop are intercepted (in case if platform doesn't define
3482 // its own hijacking listener or if the process is created by the target
3483 // manually, without the platform).
3484 if (!launch_info.GetHijackListener())
3485 launch_info.SetHijackListener(Listener::MakeListener(
3486 Process::LaunchSynchronousHijackListenerName.data()));
3487
3488 // If we're not already connected to the process, and if we have a platform
3489 // that can launch a process for debugging, go ahead and do that here.
3490 if (state != eStateConnected && platform_sp &&
3491 platform_sp->CanDebugProcess() && !launch_info.IsScriptedProcess()) {
3492 LLDB_LOGF(log, "Target::%s asking the platform to debug the process",
3493 __FUNCTION__);
3494
3495 // If there was a previous process, delete it before we make the new one.
3496 // One subtle point, we delete the process before we release the reference
3497 // to m_process_sp. That way even if we are the last owner, the process
3498 // will get Finalized before it gets destroyed.
3499 DeleteCurrentProcess();
3500
3501 m_process_sp =
3502 GetPlatform()->DebugProcess(launch_info, debugger, *this, error);
3503
3504 } else {
3505 LLDB_LOGF(log,
3506 "Target::%s the platform doesn't know how to debug a "
3507 "process, getting a process plugin to do this for us.",
3508 __FUNCTION__);
3509
3510 if (state == eStateConnected) {
3511 assert(m_process_sp);
3512 } else {
3513 // Use a Process plugin to construct the process.
3514 CreateProcess(launch_info.GetListener(),
3515 launch_info.GetProcessPluginName(), nullptr, false);
3516 }
3517
3518 // Since we didn't have a platform launch the process, launch it here.
3519 if (m_process_sp) {
3520 m_process_sp->HijackProcessEvents(launch_info.GetHijackListener());
3521 m_process_sp->SetShadowListener(launch_info.GetShadowListener());
3522 error = m_process_sp->Launch(launch_info);
3523 }
3524 }
3525
3526 if (!error.Success())
3527 return error;
3528
3529 if (!m_process_sp)
3530 return Status::FromErrorString("failed to launch or debug process");
3531
3532 bool rebroadcast_first_stop =
3533 !synchronous_execution &&
3534 launch_info.GetFlags().Test(eLaunchFlagStopAtEntry);
3535
3536 assert(launch_info.GetHijackListener());
3537
3538 EventSP first_stop_event_sp;
3539 state = m_process_sp->WaitForProcessToStop(std::nullopt, &first_stop_event_sp,
3540 rebroadcast_first_stop,
3541 launch_info.GetHijackListener());
3542 m_process_sp->RestoreProcessEvents();
3543
3544 if (rebroadcast_first_stop) {
3545 // We don't need to run the stop hooks by hand here, they will get
3546 // triggered when this rebroadcast event gets fetched.
3547 assert(first_stop_event_sp);
3548 m_process_sp->BroadcastEvent(first_stop_event_sp);
3549 return error;
3550 }
3551 // Run the stop hooks that want to run at entry.
3552 RunStopHooks(true /* at entry point */);
3553
3554 switch (state) {
3555 case eStateStopped: {
3556 if (launch_info.GetFlags().Test(eLaunchFlagStopAtEntry))
3557 break;
3558 if (synchronous_execution)
3559 // Now we have handled the stop-from-attach, and we are just
3560 // switching to a synchronous resume. So we should switch to the
3561 // SyncResume hijacker.
3562 m_process_sp->ResumeSynchronous(stream);
3563 else
3564 error = m_process_sp->Resume();
3565 if (!error.Success()) {
3566 error = Status::FromErrorStringWithFormat(
3567 "process resume at entry point failed: %s", error.AsCString());
3568 }
3569 } break;
3570 case eStateExited: {
3571 bool with_shell = !!launch_info.GetShell();
3572 const int exit_status = m_process_sp->GetExitStatus();
3573 const char *exit_desc = m_process_sp->GetExitDescription();
3574 std::string desc;
3575 if (exit_desc && exit_desc[0])
3576 desc = " (" + std::string(exit_desc) + ')';
3577 if (with_shell)
3578 error = Status::FromErrorStringWithFormat(
3579 "process exited with status %i%s\n"
3580 "'r' and 'run' are aliases that default to launching through a "
3581 "shell.\n"
3582 "Try launching without going through a shell by using "
3583 "'process launch'.",
3584 exit_status, desc.c_str());
3585 else
3586 error = Status::FromErrorStringWithFormat(
3587 "process exited with status %i%s", exit_status, desc.c_str());
3588 } break;
3589 default:
3590 error = Status::FromErrorStringWithFormat(
3591 "initial process state wasn't stopped: %s", StateAsCString(state));
3592 break;
3593 }
3594 return error;
3595}
3596
3597void Target::SetTrace(const TraceSP &trace_sp) { m_trace_sp = trace_sp; }
3598
3599TraceSP Target::GetTrace() { return m_trace_sp; }
3600
3601llvm::Expected<TraceSP> Target::CreateTrace() {
3602 if (!m_process_sp)
3603 return llvm::createStringError(llvm::inconvertibleErrorCode(),
3604 "A process is required for tracing");
3605 if (m_trace_sp)
3606 return llvm::createStringError(llvm::inconvertibleErrorCode(),
3607 "A trace already exists for the target");
3608
3609 llvm::Expected<TraceSupportedResponse> trace_type =
3610 m_process_sp->TraceSupported();
3611 if (!trace_type)
3612 return llvm::createStringError(
3613 llvm::inconvertibleErrorCode(), "Tracing is not supported. %s",
3614 llvm::toString(trace_type.takeError()).c_str());
3615 if (llvm::Expected<TraceSP> trace_sp =
3616 Trace::FindPluginForLiveProcess(trace_type->name, *m_process_sp))
3617 m_trace_sp = *trace_sp;
3618 else
3619 return llvm::createStringError(
3620 llvm::inconvertibleErrorCode(),
3621 "Couldn't create a Trace object for the process. %s",
3622 llvm::toString(trace_sp.takeError()).c_str());
3623 return m_trace_sp;
3624}
3625
3626llvm::Expected<TraceSP> Target::GetTraceOrCreate() {
3627 if (m_trace_sp)
3628 return m_trace_sp;
3629 return CreateTrace();
3630}
3631
3632Status Target::Attach(ProcessAttachInfo &attach_info, Stream *stream) {
3633 Progress attach_progress("Waiting to attach to process");
3634 m_stats.SetLaunchOrAttachTime();
3635 auto state = eStateInvalid;
3636 auto process_sp = GetProcessSP();
3637 if (process_sp) {
3638 state = process_sp->GetState();
3639 if (process_sp->IsAlive() && state != eStateConnected) {
3640 if (state == eStateAttaching)
3641 return Status::FromErrorString("process attach is in progress");
3642 return Status::FromErrorString("a process is already being debugged");
3643 }
3644 }
3645
3646 const ModuleSP old_exec_module_sp = GetExecutableModule();
3647
3648 // If no process info was specified, then use the target executable name as
3649 // the process to attach to by default
3650 if (!attach_info.ProcessInfoSpecified()) {
3651 if (old_exec_module_sp)
3652 attach_info.GetExecutableFile().SetFilename(
3653 old_exec_module_sp->GetPlatformFileSpec().GetFilename());
3654
3655 if (!attach_info.ProcessInfoSpecified()) {
3656 return Status::FromErrorString(
3657 "no process specified, create a target with a file, or "
3658 "specify the --pid or --name");
3659 }
3660 }
3661
3662 const auto platform_sp =
3663 GetDebugger().GetPlatformList().GetSelectedPlatform();
3664 ListenerSP hijack_listener_sp;
3665 const bool async = attach_info.GetAsync();
3666 if (!async) {
3667 hijack_listener_sp = Listener::MakeListener(
3668 Process::AttachSynchronousHijackListenerName.data());
3669 attach_info.SetHijackListener(hijack_listener_sp);
3670 }
3671
3672 Status error;
3673 if (state != eStateConnected && platform_sp != nullptr &&
3674 platform_sp->CanDebugProcess() && !attach_info.IsScriptedProcess()) {
3675 SetPlatform(platform_sp);
3676 process_sp = platform_sp->Attach(attach_info, GetDebugger(), this, error);
3677 } else {
3678 if (state != eStateConnected) {
3679 SaveScriptedLaunchInfo(attach_info);
3680 llvm::StringRef plugin_name = attach_info.GetProcessPluginName();
3681 process_sp =
3682 CreateProcess(attach_info.GetListenerForProcess(GetDebugger()),
3683 plugin_name, nullptr, false);
3684 if (!process_sp) {
3685 error = Status::FromErrorStringWithFormatv(
3686 "failed to create process using plugin '{0}'",
3687 plugin_name.empty() ? "<empty>" : plugin_name);
3688 return error;
3689 }
3690 }
3691 if (hijack_listener_sp)
3692 process_sp->HijackProcessEvents(hijack_listener_sp);
3693 error = process_sp->Attach(attach_info);
3694 }
3695
3696 if (error.Success() && process_sp) {
3697 if (async) {
3698 process_sp->RestoreProcessEvents();
3699 } else {
3700 // We are stopping all the way out to the user, so update selected frames.
3701 state = process_sp->WaitForProcessToStop(
3702 std::nullopt, nullptr, false, attach_info.GetHijackListener(), stream,
3703 true, SelectMostRelevantFrame);
3704 process_sp->RestoreProcessEvents();
3705
3706 // Run the stop hooks here. Since we were hijacking the events, they
3707 // wouldn't have gotten run as part of event delivery.
3708 RunStopHooks(/* at_initial_stop= */ true);
3709
3710 if (state != eStateStopped) {
3711 const char *exit_desc = process_sp->GetExitDescription();
3712 if (exit_desc)
3713 error = Status::FromErrorStringWithFormat("%s", exit_desc);
3714 else
3715 error = Status::FromErrorString(
3716 "process did not stop (no such process or permission problem?)");
3717 process_sp->Destroy(false);
3718 }
3719 }
3720 }
3721 return error;
3722}
3723
3724llvm::Expected<uint32_t> Target::AddScriptedFrameProviderDescriptor(
3725 const ScriptedFrameProviderDescriptor &descriptor) {
3726 if (!descriptor.IsValid())
3727 return llvm::createStringError("invalid frame provider descriptor");
3728
3729 uint32_t descriptor_id = descriptor.GetID();
3730
3731 llvm::StringRef name = descriptor.GetName();
3732 if (name.empty())
3733 return llvm::createStringError(
3734 "frame provider descriptor has no class name");
3735
3736 {
3737 std::unique_lock<std::recursive_mutex> guard(
3738 m_frame_provider_descriptors_mutex);
3739 m_frame_provider_descriptors[descriptor_id] = descriptor;
3740 }
3741
3742 InvalidateThreadFrameProviders();
3743
3744 return descriptor_id;
3745}
3746
3747bool Target::RemoveScriptedFrameProviderDescriptor(uint32_t id) {
3748 bool removed = false;
3749 {
3750 std::lock_guard<std::recursive_mutex> guard(
3751 m_frame_provider_descriptors_mutex);
3752 removed = m_frame_provider_descriptors.erase(id);
3753 }
3754
3755 if (removed)
3756 InvalidateThreadFrameProviders();
3757 return removed;
3758}
3759
3760void Target::ClearScriptedFrameProviderDescriptors() {
3761 {
3762 std::lock_guard<std::recursive_mutex> guard(
3763 m_frame_provider_descriptors_mutex);
3764 m_frame_provider_descriptors.clear();
3765 }
3766
3767 InvalidateThreadFrameProviders();
3768}
3769
3770const llvm::DenseMap<uint32_t, ScriptedFrameProviderDescriptor> &
3771Target::GetScriptedFrameProviderDescriptors() const {
3772 std::lock_guard<std::recursive_mutex> guard(
3773 m_frame_provider_descriptors_mutex);
3774 return m_frame_provider_descriptors;
3775}
3776
3777void Target::InvalidateThreadFrameProviders() {
3778 ProcessSP process_sp = GetProcessSP();
3779 if (!process_sp)
3780 return;
3781 for (ThreadSP thread_sp : process_sp->Threads()) {
3782 // Clear frame providers on existing threads so they reload with new config.
3783 thread_sp->ClearScriptedFrameProvider();
3784 // Notify threads that the stack traces might have changed.
3785 if (thread_sp->EventTypeHasListeners(Thread::eBroadcastBitStackChanged)) {
3786 auto data_sp = std::make_shared<Thread::ThreadEventData>(thread_sp);
3787 thread_sp->BroadcastEvent(Thread::eBroadcastBitStackChanged, data_sp);
3788 }
3789 }
3790}
3791
3792void Target::FinalizeFileActions(ProcessLaunchInfo &info) {
3793 Log *log = GetLog(LLDBLog::Process);
3794
3795 // Finalize the file actions, and if none were given, default to opening up a
3796 // pseudo terminal
3797 PlatformSP platform_sp = GetPlatform();
3798 const bool default_to_use_pty =
3799 m_platform_sp ? m_platform_sp->IsHost() : false;
3800 LLDB_LOG(
3801 log,
3802 "have platform={0}, platform_sp->IsHost()={1}, default_to_use_pty={2}",
3803 bool(platform_sp),
3804 platform_sp ? (platform_sp->IsHost() ? "true" : "false") : "n/a",
3805 default_to_use_pty);
3806
3807 // If nothing for stdin or stdout or stderr was specified, then check the
3808 // process for any default settings that were set with "settings set"
3809 if (info.GetFileActionForFD(STDIN_FILENO) == nullptr ||
3810 info.GetFileActionForFD(STDOUT_FILENO) == nullptr ||
3811 info.GetFileActionForFD(STDERR_FILENO) == nullptr) {
3812 LLDB_LOG(log, "at least one of stdin/stdout/stderr was not set, evaluating "
3813 "default handling");
3814
3815 if (info.GetFlags().Test(eLaunchFlagLaunchInTTY)) {
3816 // Do nothing, if we are launching in a remote terminal no file actions
3817 // should be done at all.
3818 return;
3819 }
3820
3821 if (info.GetFlags().Test(eLaunchFlagDisableSTDIO)) {
3822 LLDB_LOG(log, "eLaunchFlagDisableSTDIO set, adding suppression action "
3823 "for stdin, stdout and stderr");
3824 info.AppendSuppressFileAction(STDIN_FILENO, true, false);
3825 info.AppendSuppressFileAction(STDOUT_FILENO, false, true);
3826 info.AppendSuppressFileAction(STDERR_FILENO, false, true);
3827 } else {
3828 // Check for any values that might have gotten set with any of: (lldb)
3829 // settings set target.input-path (lldb) settings set target.output-path
3830 // (lldb) settings set target.error-path
3831 FileSpec in_file_spec;
3832 FileSpec out_file_spec;
3833 FileSpec err_file_spec;
3834 // Only override with the target settings if we don't already have an
3835 // action for in, out or error
3836 if (info.GetFileActionForFD(STDIN_FILENO) == nullptr)
3837 in_file_spec = GetStandardInputPath();
3838 if (info.GetFileActionForFD(STDOUT_FILENO) == nullptr)
3839 out_file_spec = GetStandardOutputPath();
3840 if (info.GetFileActionForFD(STDERR_FILENO) == nullptr)
3841 err_file_spec = GetStandardErrorPath();
3842
3843 LLDB_LOG(log, "target stdin='{0}', target stdout='{1}', stderr='{2}'",
3844 in_file_spec, out_file_spec, err_file_spec);
3845
3846 if (in_file_spec) {
3847 info.AppendOpenFileAction(STDIN_FILENO, in_file_spec, true, false);
3848 LLDB_LOG(log, "appended stdin open file action for {0}", in_file_spec);
3849 }
3850
3851 if (out_file_spec) {
3852 info.AppendOpenFileAction(STDOUT_FILENO, out_file_spec, false, true);
3853 LLDB_LOG(log, "appended stdout open file action for {0}",
3854 out_file_spec);
3855 }
3856
3857 if (err_file_spec) {
3858 info.AppendOpenFileAction(STDERR_FILENO, err_file_spec, false, true);
3859 LLDB_LOG(log, "appended stderr open file action for {0}",
3860 err_file_spec);
3861 }
3862
3863 if (default_to_use_pty) {
3864 llvm::Error Err = info.SetUpPtyRedirection();
3865 LLDB_LOG_ERROR(log, std::move(Err), "SetUpPtyRedirection failed: {0}");
3866 }
3867 }
3868 }
3869}
3870
3871void Target::AddDummySignal(llvm::StringRef name, LazyBool pass, LazyBool notify,
3872 LazyBool stop) {
3873 if (name.empty())
3874 return;
3875 // Don't add a signal if all the actions are trivial:
3876 if (pass == eLazyBoolCalculate && notify == eLazyBoolCalculate
3877 && stop == eLazyBoolCalculate)
3878 return;
3879
3880 auto& elem = m_dummy_signals[name];
3881 elem.pass = pass;
3882 elem.notify = notify;
3883 elem.stop = stop;
3884}
3885
3886bool Target::UpdateSignalFromDummy(UnixSignalsSP signals_sp,
3887 const DummySignalElement &elem) {
3888 if (!signals_sp)
3889 return false;
3890
3891 int32_t signo
3892 = signals_sp->GetSignalNumberFromName(elem.first().str().c_str());
3893 if (signo == LLDB_INVALID_SIGNAL_NUMBER)
3894 return false;
3895
3896 if (elem.second.pass == eLazyBoolYes)
3897 signals_sp->SetShouldSuppress(signo, false);
3898 else if (elem.second.pass == eLazyBoolNo)
3899 signals_sp->SetShouldSuppress(signo, true);
3900
3901 if (elem.second.notify == eLazyBoolYes)
3902 signals_sp->SetShouldNotify(signo, true);
3903 else if (elem.second.notify == eLazyBoolNo)
3904 signals_sp->SetShouldNotify(signo, false);
3905
3906 if (elem.second.stop == eLazyBoolYes)
3907 signals_sp->SetShouldStop(signo, true);
3908 else if (elem.second.stop == eLazyBoolNo)
3909 signals_sp->SetShouldStop(signo, false);
3910 return true;
3911}
3912
3913bool Target::ResetSignalFromDummy(UnixSignalsSP signals_sp,
3914 const DummySignalElement &elem) {
3915 if (!signals_sp)
3916 return false;
3917 int32_t signo
3918 = signals_sp->GetSignalNumberFromName(elem.first().str().c_str());
3919 if (signo == LLDB_INVALID_SIGNAL_NUMBER)
3920 return false;
3921 bool do_pass = elem.second.pass != eLazyBoolCalculate;
3922 bool do_stop = elem.second.stop != eLazyBoolCalculate;
3923 bool do_notify = elem.second.notify != eLazyBoolCalculate;
3924 signals_sp->ResetSignal(signo, do_stop, do_notify, do_pass);
3925 return true;
3926}
3927
3928void Target::UpdateSignalsFromDummy(UnixSignalsSP signals_sp,
3929 StreamSP warning_stream_sp) {
3930 if (!signals_sp)
3931 return;
3932
3933 for (const auto &elem : m_dummy_signals) {
3934 if (!UpdateSignalFromDummy(signals_sp, elem))
3935 warning_stream_sp->Printf("Target signal '%s' not found in process\n",
3936 elem.first().str().c_str());
3937 }
3938}
3939
3940void Target::ClearDummySignals(Args &signal_names) {
3941 ProcessSP process_sp = GetProcessSP();
3942 // The simplest case, delete them all with no process to update.
3943 if (signal_names.GetArgumentCount() == 0 && !process_sp) {
3944 m_dummy_signals.clear();
3945 return;
3946 }
3947 UnixSignalsSP signals_sp;
3948 if (process_sp)
3949 signals_sp = process_sp->GetUnixSignals();
3950
3951 for (const Args::ArgEntry &entry : signal_names) {
3952 const char *signal_name = entry.c_str();
3953 auto elem = m_dummy_signals.find(signal_name);
3954 // If we didn't find it go on.
3955 // FIXME: Should I pipe error handling through here?
3956 if (elem == m_dummy_signals.end()) {
3957 continue;
3958 }
3959 if (signals_sp)
3960 ResetSignalFromDummy(signals_sp, *elem);
3961 m_dummy_signals.erase(elem);
3962 }
3963}
3964
3965void Target::PrintDummySignals(Stream &strm, Args &signal_args) {
3966 strm.Printf("NAME PASS STOP NOTIFY\n");
3967 strm.Printf("=========== ======= ======= =======\n");
3968
3969 auto str_for_lazy = [] (LazyBool lazy) -> const char * {
3970 switch (lazy) {
3971 case eLazyBoolCalculate: return "not set";
3972 case eLazyBoolYes: return "true ";
3973 case eLazyBoolNo: return "false ";
3974 }
3975 llvm_unreachable("Fully covered switch above!");
3976 };
3977 size_t num_args = signal_args.GetArgumentCount();
3978 for (const auto &elem : m_dummy_signals) {
3979 bool print_it = false;
3980 for (size_t idx = 0; idx < num_args; idx++) {
3981 if (elem.first() == signal_args.GetArgumentAtIndex(idx)) {
3982 print_it = true;
3983 break;
3984 }
3985 }
3986 if (print_it) {
3987 strm.Printf("%-11s ", elem.first().str().c_str());
3988 strm.Printf("%s %s %s\n", str_for_lazy(elem.second.pass),
3989 str_for_lazy(elem.second.stop),
3990 str_for_lazy(elem.second.notify));
3991 }
3992 }
3993}
3994
3995// Target::StopHook
3996Target::StopHook::StopHook(lldb::TargetSP target_sp, lldb::user_id_t uid)
3997 : UserID(uid), m_target_sp(target_sp), m_specifier_sp(),
3998 m_thread_spec_up() {}
3999
4000Target::StopHook::StopHook(const StopHook &rhs)
4001 : UserID(rhs.GetID()), m_target_sp(rhs.m_target_sp),
4002 m_specifier_sp(rhs.m_specifier_sp), m_thread_spec_up(),
4003 m_active(rhs.m_active), m_auto_continue(rhs.m_auto_continue) {
4004 if (rhs.m_thread_spec_up)
4005 m_thread_spec_up = std::make_unique<ThreadSpec>(*rhs.m_thread_spec_up);
4006}
4007
4008void Target::StopHook::SetSpecifier(SymbolContextSpecifier *specifier) {
4009 m_specifier_sp.reset(specifier);
4010}
4011
4012void Target::StopHook::SetThreadSpecifier(ThreadSpec *specifier) {
4013 m_thread_spec_up.reset(specifier);
4014}
4015
4016bool Target::StopHook::ExecutionContextPasses(const ExecutionContext &exc_ctx) {
4017 SymbolContextSpecifier *specifier = GetSpecifier();
4018 if (!specifier)
4019 return true;
4020
4021 bool will_run = true;
4022 if (exc_ctx.GetFramePtr())
4023 will_run = GetSpecifier()->SymbolContextMatches(
4024 exc_ctx.GetFramePtr()->GetSymbolContext(eSymbolContextEverything));
4025 if (will_run && GetThreadSpecifier() != nullptr)
4026 will_run =
4027 GetThreadSpecifier()->ThreadPassesBasicTests(exc_ctx.GetThreadRef());
4028
4029 return will_run;
4030}
4031
4032void Target::StopHook::GetDescription(Stream &s,
4033 lldb::DescriptionLevel level) const {
4034
4035 // For brief descriptions, only print the subclass description:
4036 if (level == eDescriptionLevelBrief) {
4037 GetSubclassDescription(s, level);
4038 return;
4039 }
4040
4041 auto indent_scope = s.MakeIndentScope();
4042
4043 s.Printf("Hook: %" PRIu64 "\n", GetID());
4044 if (m_active)
4045 s.Indent("State: enabled\n");
4046 else
4047 s.Indent("State: disabled\n");
4048
4049 if (m_auto_continue)
4050 s.Indent("AutoContinue on\n");
4051
4052 if (m_specifier_sp) {
4053 s.Indent();
4054 s.PutCString("Specifier:\n");
4055 auto indent_scope = s.MakeIndentScope();
4056 m_specifier_sp->GetDescription(&s, level);
4057 }
4058
4059 if (m_thread_spec_up) {
4060 StreamString tmp;
4061 s.Indent("Thread:\n");
4062 m_thread_spec_up->GetDescription(&tmp, level);
4063 auto indent_scope = s.MakeIndentScope();
4064 s.Indent(tmp.GetString());
4065 s.PutCString("\n");
4066 }
4067 GetSubclassDescription(s, level);
4068}
4069
4070void Target::StopHookCommandLine::GetSubclassDescription(
4071 Stream &s, lldb::DescriptionLevel level) const {
4072 // The brief description just prints the first command.
4073 if (level == eDescriptionLevelBrief) {
4074 if (m_commands.GetSize() == 1)
4075 s.PutCString(m_commands.GetStringAtIndex(0));
4076 return;
4077 }
4078 s.Indent("Commands:\n");
4079 auto indent_scope = s.MakeIndentScope(4);
4080 uint32_t num_commands = m_commands.GetSize();
4081 for (uint32_t i = 0; i < num_commands; i++) {
4082 s.Indent(m_commands.GetStringAtIndex(i));
4083 s.PutCString("\n");
4084 }
4085}
4086
4087// Target::StopHookCommandLine
4088void Target::StopHookCommandLine::SetActionFromString(const std::string &string) {
4089 GetCommands().SplitIntoLines(string);
4090}
4091
4092void Target::StopHookCommandLine::SetActionFromStrings(
4093 const std::vector<std::string> &strings) {
4094 for (auto string : strings)
4095 GetCommands().AppendString(string.c_str());
4096}
4097
4098Target::StopHook::StopHookResult
4099Target::StopHookCommandLine::HandleStop(ExecutionContext &exc_ctx,
4100 StreamSP output_sp) {
4101 assert(exc_ctx.GetTargetPtr() && "Can't call PerformAction on a context "
4102 "with no target");
4103
4104 if (!m_commands.GetSize())
4105 return StopHookResult::KeepStopped;
4106
4107 CommandReturnObject result(false);
4108 result.SetImmediateOutputStream(output_sp);
4109 result.SetInteractive(false);
4110 Debugger &debugger = exc_ctx.GetTargetPtr()->GetDebugger();
4111 CommandInterpreterRunOptions options;
4112 options.SetStopOnContinue(true);
4113 options.SetStopOnError(true);
4114 options.SetEchoCommands(false);
4115 options.SetPrintResults(true);
4116 options.SetPrintErrors(true);
4117 options.SetAddToHistory(false);
4118
4119 // Force Async:
4120 bool old_async = debugger.GetAsyncExecution();
4121 debugger.SetAsyncExecution(true);
4122 debugger.GetCommandInterpreter().HandleCommands(GetCommands(), exc_ctx,
4123 options, result);
4124 debugger.SetAsyncExecution(old_async);
4125 lldb::ReturnStatus status = result.GetStatus();
4126 if (status == eReturnStatusSuccessContinuingNoResult ||
4127 status == eReturnStatusSuccessContinuingResult)
4128 return StopHookResult::AlreadyContinued;
4129 return StopHookResult::KeepStopped;
4130}
4131
4132// Target::StopHookScripted
4133Status Target::StopHookScripted::SetScriptCallback(
4134 std::string class_name, StructuredData::ObjectSP extra_args_sp) {
4135 Status error;
4136
4137 ScriptInterpreter *script_interp =
4138 GetTarget()->GetDebugger().GetScriptInterpreter();
4139 if (!script_interp) {
4140 error = Status::FromErrorString("No script interpreter installed.");
4141 return error;
4142 }
4143
4144 m_interface_sp = script_interp->CreateScriptedStopHookInterface();
4145 if (!m_interface_sp) {
4146 error = Status::FromErrorStringWithFormat(
4147 "ScriptedStopHook::%s () - ERROR: %s", __FUNCTION__,
4148 "Script interpreter couldn't create Scripted Stop Hook Interface");
4149 return error;
4150 }
4151
4152 m_class_name = class_name;
4153 m_extra_args.SetObjectSP(extra_args_sp);
4154
4155 auto obj_or_err = m_interface_sp->CreatePluginObject(
4156 m_class_name, GetTarget(), m_extra_args);
4157 if (!obj_or_err) {
4158 return Status::FromError(obj_or_err.takeError());
4159 }
4160
4161 StructuredData::ObjectSP object_sp = *obj_or_err;
4162 if (!object_sp || !object_sp->IsValid()) {
4163 error = Status::FromErrorStringWithFormat(
4164 "ScriptedStopHook::%s () - ERROR: %s", __FUNCTION__,
4165 "Failed to create valid script object");
4166 return error;
4167 }
4168
4169 return {};
4170}
4171
4172Target::StopHook::StopHookResult
4173Target::StopHookScripted::HandleStop(ExecutionContext &exc_ctx,
4174 StreamSP output_sp) {
4175 assert(exc_ctx.GetTargetPtr() && "Can't call HandleStop on a context "
4176 "with no target");
4177
4178 if (!m_interface_sp)
4179 return StopHookResult::KeepStopped;
4180
4181 lldb::StreamSP stream = std::make_shared<lldb_private::StreamString>();
4182 auto should_stop_or_err = m_interface_sp->HandleStop(exc_ctx, stream);
4183 output_sp->PutCString(
4184 reinterpret_cast<StreamString *>(stream.get())->GetData());
4185 if (!should_stop_or_err)
4186 return StopHookResult::KeepStopped;
4187
4188 return *should_stop_or_err ? StopHookResult::KeepStopped
4189 : StopHookResult::RequestContinue;
4190}
4191
4192void Target::StopHookScripted::GetSubclassDescription(
4193 Stream &s, lldb::DescriptionLevel level) const {
4194 if (level == eDescriptionLevelBrief) {
4195 s.PutCString(m_class_name);
4196 return;
4197 }
4198 s.Indent("Class:");
4199 s.Printf("%s\n", m_class_name.c_str());
4200
4201 // Now print the extra args:
4202 // FIXME: We should use StructuredData.GetDescription on the m_extra_args
4203 // but that seems to rely on some printing plugin that doesn't exist.
4204 if (!m_extra_args.IsValid())
4205 return;
4206 StructuredData::ObjectSP object_sp = m_extra_args.GetObjectSP();
4207 if (!object_sp || !object_sp->IsValid())
4208 return;
4209
4210 StructuredData::Dictionary *as_dict = object_sp->GetAsDictionary();
4211 if (!as_dict || !as_dict->IsValid())
4212 return;
4213
4214 uint32_t num_keys = as_dict->GetSize();
4215 if (num_keys == 0)
4216 return;
4217
4218 s.Indent("Args:\n");
4219 auto indent_scope = s.MakeIndentScope(4);
4220
4221 auto print_one_element = [&s](llvm::StringRef key,
4222 StructuredData::Object *object) {
4223 s.Indent();
4224 s.Format("{0} : {1}\n", key, object->GetStringValue());
4225 return true;
4226 };
4227
4228 as_dict->ForEach(print_one_element);
4229}
4230
4231static constexpr OptionEnumValueElement g_dynamic_value_types[] = {
4232 {
4233 eNoDynamicValues,
4234 "no-dynamic-values",
4235 "Don't calculate the dynamic type of values",
4236 },
4237 {
4238 eDynamicCanRunTarget,
4239 "run-target",
4240 "Calculate the dynamic type of values "
4241 "even if you have to run the target.",
4242 },
4243 {
4244 eDynamicDontRunTarget,
4245 "no-run-target",
4246 "Calculate the dynamic type of values, but don't run the target.",
4247 },
4248};
4249
4250OptionEnumValues lldb_private::GetDynamicValueTypes() {
4251 return OptionEnumValues(g_dynamic_value_types);
4252}

4254static constexpr OptionEnumValueElement g_inline_breakpoint_enums[] = {
4255 {
4256 eInlineBreakpointsNever,
4257 "never",
4258 "Never look for inline breakpoint locations (fastest). This setting "
4259 "should only be used if you know that no inlining occurs in your"
4260 "programs.",
4261 },
4262 {
4263 eInlineBreakpointsHeaders,
4264 "headers",
4265 "Only check for inline breakpoint locations when setting breakpoints "
4266 "in header files, but not when setting breakpoint in implementation "
4267 "source files (default).",
4268 },
4269 {
4270 eInlineBreakpointsAlways,
4271 "always",
4272 "Always look for inline breakpoint locations when setting file and "
4273 "line breakpoints (slower but most accurate).",
4274 },
4275};

4310 "Retry evaluating expressions with an imported 'std' C++ module if they"
4311 " failed to parse without the module. This allows evaluating more "

4324 g_dynamic_class_info_helper_value_types[] = {

4393 "this setting loads sections only.",
4394 },

4397 "partial",
4398 "Load partial information when loading modules from memory. Currently "
4399 "this setting loads sections and function bounds.",
4400 },

4409#define LLDB_PROPERTIES_target
4410#include "TargetProperties.inc"
4411
4412enum {
4413#define LLDB_PROPERTIES_target
4414#include "TargetPropertiesEnum.inc"
4415 ePropertyExperimental,
4416};
4417
4418class TargetOptionValueProperties
4419 : public Cloneable<TargetOptionValueProperties, OptionValueProperties> {
4420public:
4421 TargetOptionValueProperties(llvm::StringRef name) : Cloneable(name) {}
4422
4423 const Property *
4424 GetPropertyAtIndex(size_t idx,
4425 const ExecutionContext *exe_ctx = nullptr) const override {
4426 // When getting the value for a key from the target options, we will always
4427 // try and grab the setting from the current target if there is one. Else
4428 // we just use the one from this instance.
4429 if (exe_ctx) {
4430 Target *target = exe_ctx->GetTargetPtr();
4431 if (target) {
4432 TargetOptionValueProperties *target_properties =
4433 static_cast<TargetOptionValueProperties *>(
4434 target->GetValueProperties().get());
4435 if (this != target_properties)
4436 return target_properties->ProtectedGetPropertyAtIndex(idx);
4437 }
4438 }
4439 return ProtectedGetPropertyAtIndex(idx);
4440 }
4441};
4442
4443// TargetProperties
4444#define LLDB_PROPERTIES_target_experimental
4445#include "TargetProperties.inc"
4446
4447enum {
4448#define LLDB_PROPERTIES_target_experimental
4449#include "TargetPropertiesEnum.inc"
4450};
4451
4452class TargetExperimentalOptionValueProperties
4453 : public Cloneable<TargetExperimentalOptionValueProperties,
4454 OptionValueProperties> {
4455public:
4456 TargetExperimentalOptionValueProperties()
4457 : Cloneable(Properties::GetExperimentalSettingsName()) {}
4458};
4459
4460TargetExperimentalProperties::TargetExperimentalProperties()
4461 : Properties(OptionValuePropertiesSP(
4462 new TargetExperimentalOptionValueProperties())) {
4463 m_collection_sp->Initialize(g_target_experimental_properties_def);
4464}
4465
4466// TargetProperties
4467TargetProperties::TargetProperties(Target *target)
4468 : Properties(), m_launch_info(), m_target(target) {
4469 if (target) {
4470 m_collection_sp =
4471 OptionValueProperties::CreateLocalCopy(Target::GetGlobalProperties());
4472
4473 // Set callbacks to update launch_info whenever "settins set" updated any
4474 // of these properties
4475 m_collection_sp->SetValueChangedCallback(
4476 ePropertyArg0, [this] { Arg0ValueChangedCallback(); });
4477 m_collection_sp->SetValueChangedCallback(
4478 ePropertyRunArgs, [this] { RunArgsValueChangedCallback(); });
4479 m_collection_sp->SetValueChangedCallback(
4480 ePropertyEnvVars, [this] { EnvVarsValueChangedCallback(); });
4481 m_collection_sp->SetValueChangedCallback(
4482 ePropertyUnsetEnvVars, [this] { EnvVarsValueChangedCallback(); });
4483 m_collection_sp->SetValueChangedCallback(
4484 ePropertyInheritEnv, [this] { EnvVarsValueChangedCallback(); });
4485 m_collection_sp->SetValueChangedCallback(
4486 ePropertyInputPath, [this] { InputPathValueChangedCallback(); });
4487 m_collection_sp->SetValueChangedCallback(
4488 ePropertyOutputPath, [this] { OutputPathValueChangedCallback(); });
4489 m_collection_sp->SetValueChangedCallback(
4490 ePropertyErrorPath, [this] { ErrorPathValueChangedCallback(); });
4491 m_collection_sp->SetValueChangedCallback(ePropertyDetachOnError, [this] {
4492 DetachOnErrorValueChangedCallback();
4493 });
4494 m_collection_sp->SetValueChangedCallback(
4495 ePropertyDisableASLR, [this] { DisableASLRValueChangedCallback(); });
4496 m_collection_sp->SetValueChangedCallback(
4497 ePropertyInheritTCC, [this] { InheritTCCValueChangedCallback(); });
4498 m_collection_sp->SetValueChangedCallback(
4499 ePropertyDisableSTDIO, [this] { DisableSTDIOValueChangedCallback(); });
4500
4501 m_collection_sp->SetValueChangedCallback(
4502 ePropertySaveObjectsDir, [this] { CheckJITObjectsDir(); });
4503 m_experimental_properties_up =
4504 std::make_unique<TargetExperimentalProperties>();
4505 m_collection_sp->AppendProperty(
4506 Properties::GetExperimentalSettingsName(),
4507 "Experimental settings - setting these won't produce "
4508 "errors if the setting is not present.",
4509 true, m_experimental_properties_up->GetValueProperties());
4510 } else {
4511 m_collection_sp = std::make_shared<TargetOptionValueProperties>("target");
4512 m_collection_sp->Initialize(g_target_properties_def);
4513 m_experimental_properties_up =
4514 std::make_unique<TargetExperimentalProperties>();
4515 m_collection_sp->AppendProperty(
4516 Properties::GetExperimentalSettingsName(),
4517 "Experimental settings - setting these won't produce "
4518 "errors if the setting is not present.",
4519 true, m_experimental_properties_up->GetValueProperties());
4520 m_collection_sp->AppendProperty(
4521 "process", "Settings specific to processes.", true,
4522 Process::GetGlobalProperties().GetValueProperties());
4523 m_collection_sp->SetValueChangedCallback(
4524 ePropertySaveObjectsDir, [this] { CheckJITObjectsDir(); });
4525 }
4526}
4527
4528TargetProperties::~TargetProperties() = default;
4529
4530void TargetProperties::UpdateLaunchInfoFromProperties() {
4531 Arg0ValueChangedCallback();
4532 RunArgsValueChangedCallback();
4533 EnvVarsValueChangedCallback();
4534 InputPathValueChangedCallback();
4535 OutputPathValueChangedCallback();
4536 ErrorPathValueChangedCallback();
4537 DetachOnErrorValueChangedCallback();
4538 DisableASLRValueChangedCallback();
4539 InheritTCCValueChangedCallback();
4540 DisableSTDIOValueChangedCallback();
4541}
4542
4543std::optional<bool> TargetProperties::GetExperimentalPropertyValue(
4544 size_t prop_idx, ExecutionContext *exe_ctx) const {
4545 const Property *exp_property =
4546 m_collection_sp->GetPropertyAtIndex(ePropertyExperimental, exe_ctx);
4547 OptionValueProperties *exp_values =
4548 exp_property->GetValue()->GetAsProperties();
4549 if (exp_values)
4550 return exp_values->GetPropertyAtIndexAs<bool>(prop_idx, exe_ctx);
4551 return std::nullopt;
4552}
4553
4554bool TargetProperties::GetInjectLocalVariables(
4555 ExecutionContext *exe_ctx) const {
4556 return GetExperimentalPropertyValue(ePropertyInjectLocalVars, exe_ctx)
4557 .value_or(true);
4558}
4559
4560bool TargetProperties::GetUseDIL(ExecutionContext *exe_ctx) const {
4561 const Property *exp_property =
4562 m_collection_sp->GetPropertyAtIndex(ePropertyExperimental, exe_ctx);
4563 OptionValueProperties *exp_values =
4564 exp_property->GetValue()->GetAsProperties();
4565 if (exp_values)
4566 return exp_values->GetPropertyAtIndexAs<bool>(ePropertyUseDIL, exe_ctx)
4567 .value_or(false);
4568 else
4569 return true;
4570}
4571
4572void TargetProperties::SetUseDIL(ExecutionContext *exe_ctx, bool b) {
4573 const Property *exp_property =
4574 m_collection_sp->GetPropertyAtIndex(ePropertyExperimental, exe_ctx);
4575 OptionValueProperties *exp_values =
4576 exp_property->GetValue()->GetAsProperties();
4577 if (exp_values)
4578 exp_values->SetPropertyAtIndex(ePropertyUseDIL, true, exe_ctx);
4579}

4742
4743Environment TargetProperties::ComputeEnvironment() const {
4744 Environment env;
4745
4746 if (m_target &&
4747 GetPropertyAtIndexAs<bool>(
4748 ePropertyInheritEnv,
4749 g_target_properties[ePropertyInheritEnv].default_uint_value != 0)) {
4750 if (auto platform_sp = m_target->GetPlatform()) {
4751 Environment platform_env = platform_sp->GetEnvironment();
4752 for (const auto &KV : platform_env)
4753 env[KV.first()] = KV.second;
4754 }
4755 }
4756
4757 Args property_unset_env;
4758 m_collection_sp->GetPropertyAtIndexAsArgs(ePropertyUnsetEnvVars,
4759 property_unset_env);
4760 for (const auto &var : property_unset_env)
4761 env.erase(var.ref());
4762
4763 Args property_env;
4764 m_collection_sp->GetPropertyAtIndexAsArgs(ePropertyEnvVars, property_env);
4765 for (const auto &KV : Environment(property_env))
4766 env[KV.first()] = KV.second;
4767
4768 return env;
4769}
4770
4771Environment TargetProperties::GetEnvironment() const {
4772 return ComputeEnvironment();
4773}
4774
4775Environment TargetProperties::GetInheritedEnvironment() const {
4776 Environment environment;
4777
4778 if (m_target == nullptr)
4779 return environment;
4780
4781 if (!GetPropertyAtIndexAs<bool>(
4782 ePropertyInheritEnv,
4783 g_target_properties[ePropertyInheritEnv].default_uint_value != 0))
4784 return environment;
4785
4786 PlatformSP platform_sp = m_target->GetPlatform();
4787 if (platform_sp == nullptr)
4788 return environment;
4789
4790 Environment platform_environment = platform_sp->GetEnvironment();
4791 for (const auto &KV : platform_environment)
4792 environment[KV.first()] = KV.second;
4793
4794 Args property_unset_environment;
4795 m_collection_sp->GetPropertyAtIndexAsArgs(ePropertyUnsetEnvVars,
4796 property_unset_environment);
4797 for (const auto &var : property_unset_environment)
4798 environment.erase(var.ref());
4799
4800 return environment;
4801}
4802
4803Environment TargetProperties::GetTargetEnvironment() const {
4804 Args property_environment;
4805 m_collection_sp->GetPropertyAtIndexAsArgs(ePropertyEnvVars,
4806 property_environment);
4807 Environment environment;
4808 for (const auto &KV : Environment(property_environment))
4809 environment[KV.first()] = KV.second;
4810
4811 return environment;
4812}
4813
4814void TargetProperties::SetEnvironment(Environment env) {
4815 // TODO: Get rid of the Args intermediate step
4816 const uint32_t idx = ePropertyEnvVars;
4817 m_collection_sp->SetPropertyAtIndexFromArgs(idx, Args(env));
4818}
